exercises.title: Blog Data Model
exercises.description: Build the data model for a blog platform with: User, Post, Comment, and Tag entities. Implement in-memory repositories with full CRUD and relationship queries. This is the data layer for the blog capstone.
exercises.starterCode:
from datetime import datetime
from collections import defaultdict

class BlogDB:
    def __init__(self):
        self.users = {}
        self.posts = {}
        self.comments = {}
        self.tags = {}
        self.post_tags = defaultdict(set)  # {post_id: {tag_id}}
        self._counters = {"user": 1, "post": 1, "comment": 1, "tag": 1}

    def _next_id(self, entity):
        id_ = self._counters[entity]
        self._counters[entity] += 1
        return id_

    # User CRUD
    def create_user(self, username, email):
        # TODO: Create and store user dict with id, username, email, created_at
        pass

    # Post CRUD
    def create_post(self, title, body, author_id, slug=None):
        # TODO: Create post with id, title, body, author_id, slug (default: title.lower().replace(' ', '-')),
        # created_at, published=True
        pass

    def get_posts_by_author(self, author_id):
        return [p for p in self.posts.values() if p["author_id"] == author_id]

    # Comment CRUD
    def add_comment(self, post_id, author_name, body):
        # TODO: Create comment with id, post_id, author_name, body, created_at
        pass

    def get_comments_for_post(self, post_id):
        return [c for c in self.comments.values() if c["post_id"] == post_id]

    # Tag system
    def get_or_create_tag(self, name):
        # TODO: Find existing tag by name (case-insensitive) or create new one
        name_lower = name.lower()
        for tag in self.tags.values():
            if tag["name"] == name_lower:
                return tag
        # Create new
        tag = {"id": self._next_id("tag"), "name": name_lower}
        self.tags[tag["id"]] = tag
        return tag

    def tag_post(self, post_id, tag_name):
        tag = self.get_or_create_tag(tag_name)
        self.post_tags[post_id].add(tag["id"])
        return tag

    def get_tags_for_post(self, post_id):
        return [self.tags[tid] for tid in self.post_tags.get(post_id, set())]

# Build a sample blog
db = BlogDB()

# Create users
alice = db.create_user("alice", "alice@blog.com")
bob = db.create_user("bob", "bob@blog.com")
print(f"Users: {alice['username']} (id={alice['id']}), {bob['username']} (id={bob['id']})")

# Create posts
p1 = db.create_post("Introduction to Python", "Python is great...", alice["id"])
p2 = db.create_post("Flask Tutorial", "Flask is a micro framework...", alice["id"])
p3 = db.create_post("Testing Best Practices", "Test-driven development...", bob["id"])
print(f"Posts created: {len(db.posts)}")
print(f"Post 1 slug: {p1['slug']}")

# Tag posts
db.tag_post(p1["id"], "Python")
db.tag_post(p1["id"], "Beginner")
db.tag_post(p2["id"], "Python")
db.tag_post(p2["id"], "Flask")
db.tag_post(p3["id"], "Testing")

# Add comments
db.add_comment(p1["id"], "Carol", "Great intro!")
db.add_comment(p1["id"], "Dave", "Very helpful, thanks!")
db.add_comment(p2["id"], "Eve", "Flask is my favorite!")

# Query
alice_posts = db.get_posts_by_author(alice["id"])
print(f"Alice's posts: {len(alice_posts)}")

p1_tags = db.get_tags_for_post(p1["id"])
print(f"Post 1 tags: {sorted([t['name'] for t in p1_tags])}")

p1_comments = db.get_comments_for_post(p1["id"])
print(f"Post 1 comments: {len(p1_comments)}")
for c in p1_comments:
    print(f"  {c['author_name']}: {c['body']}")
exercises.expectedOutput:
Users: alice (id=1), bob (id=2)
Posts created: 3
Post 1 slug: introduction-to-python
Alice's posts: 2
Post 1 tags: ['beginner', 'python']
Post 1 comments: 2
  Carol: Great intro!
  Dave: Very helpful, thanks!
hints: create_post slug: slug = slug or title.lower().replace(' ', '-')
add_comment: similar pattern with id, post_id, author_name, body, created_at.
Tags are stored by their lowercase name — get_or_create_tag does case-insensitive lookup.

exercises.title: Blog API Layer
exercises.description: Build the business logic layer for the blog. Implement `BlogService` with: create_post (with validation), publish/unpublish, search posts by title/tag, and generate a site map. This practices the service pattern from the architecture lesson.
exercises.starterCode:
from collections import defaultdict

class BlogService:
    def __init__(self, db):
        self.db = db
        self._next_id = 1
        self.posts = {}   # Internal storage for this exercise

    def create_post(self, title, body, author_id, tags=None):
        """
        Create a post with validation.
        Raises ValueError if title is empty or body is too short (<10 chars).
        Returns the created post.
        """
        title = title.strip()
        if not title:
            raise ValueError("Post title cannot be empty")
        if len(body.strip()) < 10:
            raise ValueError("Post body must be at least 10 characters")

        slug = title.lower().replace(' ', '-')
        post = {
            'id': self._next_id,
            'title': title,
            'slug': slug,
            'body': body.strip(),
            'author_id': author_id,
            'tags': [t.lower() for t in (tags or [])],
            'published': True,
        }
        self.posts[self._next_id] = post
        self._next_id += 1
        return post

    def toggle_publish(self, post_id):
        """Toggle published status. Returns new status."""
        post = self.posts.get(post_id)
        if not post:
            raise ValueError(f"Post {post_id} not found")
        post['published'] = not post['published']
        return post['published']

    def search(self, query="", tag=None, published_only=True):
        """Search posts by title query and/or tag."""
        results = list(self.posts.values())
        if published_only:
            results = [p for p in results if p['published']]
        if query:
            q_lower = query.lower()
            results = [p for p in results if q_lower in p['title'].lower()]
        if tag:
            results = [p for p in results if tag.lower() in p['tags']]
        return results

    def sitemap(self):
        """Generate a list of public URLs."""
        return [f"/posts/{p['slug']}" for p in self.posts.values() if p['published']]

# Test
service = BlogService(None)

# Create posts
service.create_post("Python Tips", "Here are Python tips for beginners.", 1, tags=["Python", "Tips"])
service.create_post("Flask Guide", "A complete guide to Flask web framework.", 1, tags=["Python", "Flask"])
service.create_post("Testing Guide", "How to test your Python applications.", 2, tags=["Python", "Testing"])
service.create_post("Git Basics", "Version control with Git.", 2, tags=["Git", "DevOps"])

# Validation
try:
    service.create_post("", "some content here", 1)
except ValueError as e:
    print(f"Validation: {e}")

# Toggle publish
status = service.toggle_publish(3)
print(f"Post 3 published: {status}")

# Search
results = service.search(query="python")
print(f"Search 'python': {len(results)} results")
for r in results:
    print(f"  - {r['title']}")

results_by_tag = service.search(tag="Flask")
print(f"Tag 'Flask': {[r['title'] for r in results_by_tag]}")

# Sitemap (post 3 is unpublished now)
sitemap = service.sitemap()
print(f"Sitemap ({len(sitemap)} URLs):")
for url in sorted(sitemap):
    print(f"  {url}")
exercises.expectedOutput:
Validation: Post title cannot be empty
Post 3 published: False
Search 'python': 1 results
  - Python Tips
Tag 'Flask': ['Flask Guide']
Sitemap (3 URLs):
  /posts/flask-guide
  /posts/git-basics
  /posts/python-tips
hints: search checks title text case-insensitively: 'python' in 'python tips' = True, 'python' in 'flask guide' = False.
Post 3 ('Testing Guide') is unpublished after toggle_publish(3), so it's excluded from published_only search.
Sitemap excludes post 3 (unpublished): shows flask-guide, git-basics, python-tips.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: slug generation
warmup.starterCode:
titles = ["Hello World", "Python Tips & Tricks", "My First Post!"]
for t in titles:
    slug = t.lower().replace(' ', '-').replace('&', 'and').replace('!', '')
    print(slug)
